note: Self-contained: runs in a fresh session against the repo's ralph/logs + ralph/reviews ledger + git. Real exercise = run /ralph-retro after a loop batch; it reads local logs/git (gh optional for CI cross-ref).

evals.prompt: Retro the latest Ralph run and tell me what we can improve.
evals.expected_output: Runs scan.sh to find un-reviewed logs; evaluates each against the rubric (process/efficiency/correctness/guardrails) using runs.csv + git; proposes improvements routed to AGENTS.md/CONTEXT.md/PROMPT.md/ADR/script/backlog; applies only on approval; appends to ledger + writes archive review.

evals.prompt: How is the loop performing across the recent runs — any recurring problems?
evals.expected_output: Reviews only un-reviewed logs, then surfaces cross-run patterns (recurring waste/failures, cost drift) from the ledger, ranked by impact; does not re-ingest old reviews.

evals.prompt: Do a post-run review and update our learnings so the next run is smarter.
evals.expected_output: Per-run review + routes durable gotchas into AGENTS.md/CONTEXT.md (curated, deduped) and process changes into PROMPT/subagents on approval; records ledger + backlog; never pushes/closes/merges.
